context.setting: Working on laptop with laptop in Front

episode_text:
day=12; meal=general tso's chicken and broccoli; setting=Working on laptop with laptop in Front; time_of_day=evening;
preferences: microwave_time=2 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=outside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=near; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=1000 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected longer reheating for savory meals in that right-social context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front or on the left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN 2 min BECAUSE repeated working meals, including the new front-laptop savory episode, support a stable preference for moderately hotter savory food while working. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed sweet meal including brownies with sauce and fruit, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the observed working-right dessert-like meal matches the user's broader tendency to reheat mixed sweet meals moderately rather than serve them cold. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user explicitly chose not to microwave fruit-only items intended to be served cold. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed sweet meal including brownies with sauce, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user selected reheating for dessert-like items in that context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user selected the longest reheating in that observed left-TV savory meal. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a sandwich-style savory meal, THEN 1 min BECAUSE the observed right-TV meal supports a milder reheating preference for that food type in that context.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected this in right-TV contexts. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE this was selected repeatedly in right-social meals, showing a stable desire to preserve visibility toward the social partner. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE this was selected repeatedly while working with the attended device in front, including the new front-laptop meal. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user aligns occlusion avoidance with the attended device location. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE this was selected across multiple front-TV meals. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the user selected preserving visibility toward the left-side TV. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE this was selected repeatedly in left-laptop meals, suggesting occlusion concerns track the specific attended target and are not purely side-based across all work contexts.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN fast BECAUSE the user selected fast speed in that easy-to-serve, low-risk context. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed dessert meal with sauce, THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected a more moderate speed for a messier meal. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is hot savory food, THEN medium BECAUSE repeated right-social meals support a balanced speed preference in that context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is hot savory food or mixed dessert/fruit meals, THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected medium speed across observed working meals, including the new front-laptop savory episode. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN slow BECAUSE the user selected slow speed in that more cautious, messier context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a sandwich-style savory meal, THEN medium BECAUSE the observed meal reinforces a moderate speed preference in that context.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food requires fork acquisition during feeding AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is most solid savory foods, THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user has limited mouth opening and repeatedly favored narrower bites for savory solids, including the new front-laptop chicken-and-broccoli meal. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is soft dessert items such as brownies with sauce, THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected perpendicular skewering in that observed meal, suggesting willingness to prioritize bite acquisition success for soft dessert foods. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed meal including brownies and banana pieces, THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the observed episode supports the same dessert-related exception, where the user accepts different acquisition geometry to improve pickup success for soft sweets.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fork-fed solid food, THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose confirmation in that social context, consistent with wanting to avoid awkward failed transfers. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fork-fed solid food or mixed dessert/fruit meals, THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected confirmation while working, including the new front-laptop savory episode, suggesting a stable preference for explicit verification in attention-dividing contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is simple fruit pieces, THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped confirmation in that easier feeding context. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed dessert meal with sauce, THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected confirmation for a more complex, messier bite-acquisition context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected confirmation in that more complex savory feeding context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a simple sandwich-style solid meal, THEN no BECAUSE the user was comfortable skipping confirmation in that easier right-TV feeding context.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a neutral personal meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is foods without sauces or dips, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user can lean forward comfortably and selected the less intrusive transfer style. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE repeated right-social meals support the less intrusive, user-controlled transfer style. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food or mixed dessert/fruit meals, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user continued to prefer the less intrusive transfer style across working meals, including the new front-laptop savory episode. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fruit pieces or dessert foods, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user also selected outside-mouth transfer across front-TV meals. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a sandwich-style savory meal, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE this matches the user's broader preference for user-controlled, less intrusive delivery. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user selected direct delivery in that observed meal, indicating that in some side-TV savory contexts the user prioritizes reduced self-motion over the usual less-intrusive style.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context is a neutral personal meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is foods without sauces or dips, THEN near BECAUSE the user selected a close stop distance in the observed personal meal. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food, THEN medium BECAUSE repeated right-social meals support a somewhat larger buffer in social settings. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food or mixed dessert/fruit meals, THEN near BECAUSE the user selected a close stop distance across working meals, including the new front-laptop episode. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fruit pieces or dessert foods, THEN near BECAUSE the user consistently selected a close stop distance in front-TV meals. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a sandwich-style savory meal, THEN near BECAUSE the observed meal reinforces a close stop preference in that context. IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer AND dining context is any observed context AND affective_state is any observed state AND food_condition is any observed food condition, THEN not applicable BECAUSE this parameter does not apply when the user chooses inside-mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE repeated right-social meals show a stable preference for quieter signaling. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose LED during working meals, including the new front-laptop episode, consistent with low-disruption visual cues during focused activity. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE this was selected across front-TV meals, reinforcing a preference for quiet visual cues when attention is directed toward media. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected a multimodal cue in that observed meal, suggesting stronger prompting when more automation is used. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a sandwich-style savory meal, THEN speech BECAUSE the observed meal shows the user can prefer audible prompting in right-TV contexts when paired with high automation.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves possible distraction, conversation, or focused activity AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is most previously observed meals, THEN button BECAUSE the user has reliable arm control, limited mouth opening makes open-mouth readiness less attractive, and many meals favored explicit button control. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food, THEN button BECAUSE repeated right-social meals reinforce explicit manual initiation in social settings. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food or mixed dessert/fruit meals, THEN button BECAUSE the user consistently selected manual initiation across working meals, including the new front-laptop episode. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic progression in that observed meal. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a simple sandwich-style solid meal, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the observed meal shows that in TV-viewing contexts the user may accept more automation for feeding initiation. IF dining context evidence conflicts between explicit control and automation AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is TV-viewing meals, THEN favor autocontinue over button BECAUSE the observed TV meals show a shift toward lower-effort automation, while open mouth remains unattractive given limited mouth opening.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE repeated right-social meals show a stable preference for quieter completion cues. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected LED during working meals, including the new front-laptop episode. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user selected manual completion across working meals, including the new front-laptop episode. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic completion in that observed meal. retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated right-social meals show the user prioritizes reducing prolonged obstruction when attention to a right-side partner is important. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated front-working meals, including the new front-laptop savory episode, show the user wants the robot to clear the front view between tasks. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE this was selected across front-TV meals. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected retraction in the observed left-TV meal. IF dining context includes watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN no BECAUSE the observed right-TV meal shows the user may tolerate continued arm presence to reduce meal time even when attending to the right side. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the left or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN no BECAUSE repeated left-laptop meals and the observed right-laptop meal show the user does not want the extra time cost of retracting in side-working contexts. IF dining context evidence conflicts between preserving visibility and reducing time AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is side-attention media or work contexts, THEN the preference is context-specific rather than globally stable BECAUSE the user chose retraction for front-facing attention targets and left-TV, but not for right-TV or side-laptop contexts.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified, THEN less BECAUSE the user selected moderate dipping in an observed right-social savory meal with ranch dressing, consistent with a mess-conscious social preference. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context involves watching TV in front AND food_condition is simple items where dipping is optional, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user explicitly declined dipping in one observed front-TV meal despite sauce availability, showing a conservative baseline. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified, THEN less BECAUSE the user selected some dipping but not maximal dipping across several working meals with tartar sauce, ranch dressing, ketchup, and chocolate sauce, suggesting a moderate, mess-conscious preference in task-focused contexts. IF food does not include a dip or sauce AND dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a savory meal such as chicken and broccoli, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the new front-working savory episode reinforces that the user does not add dipping when it is unnecessary. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a dessert meal with chocolate sauce, THEN less BECAUSE the user selected some dipping when the sauce was integral to the meal. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a savory meal with ranch dressing, THEN less BECAUSE the user again selected moderate dipping in that observed meal. IF food_condition is a meal without dips or sauces AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND dining context is any observed context, THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is unnecessary and the user's observed choices remain conservative. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a sandwich-style meal without dipping use, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed meal is consistent with the user's conservative no-dip baseline when dipping is not needed.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE repeated right-social meals show a strong preference to avoid unwanted automatic progression when social interaction may delay responses. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front, on the left, or on the right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a very long wait while working, including the new front-laptop episode, suggesting a stable preference to prevent automatic progression when attention is divided. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is simple fruit pieces, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected the short wait in that easier front-TV meal. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed dessert meal with sauce, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very long wait in that more complex front-TV meal, indicating that in TV contexts the wait preference depends on meal complexity. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal with dip, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected a short wait in that observed meal, consistent with accepting quicker automatic progression when they had already chosen multiple automation-supporting settings. IF dining context involves watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a sandwich-style savory meal, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the observed meal reinforces a short wait preference in TV-viewing contexts where the user accepts more automation.
